message: row.production.toLocaleString() is called without guarding against null/undefined/NaN. If a production value is missing or non-numeric (0 is falsy-adjacent, null/undefined are common in partial FSA imports), this crashes the render. The adjacent moisturePercent column explicitly guards against null (row.moisturePercent != null), but production does not, creating an inconsistent and crash-prone contract for consumers.

Applying the `mobile-cards` class here relies on `<td data-label="...">` to generate labels in mobile view. However, the tables rendered directly inside ReportTable.tsx (or passed as children) may not have `data-label` attributes set on all their cells. In the mobile view (<=768px), the CSS in src/index.css forces `td::before { content: attr(data-label); }`, which renders an empty pseudo-element for any cell missing the attribute, leaving unlabeled, right-aligned data on mobile.

The `truncate max-w-[80px]` classes on DEST/STORAGE and EVIDENCE # conflict with the new mobile CSS. The CSS forces `display: flex` and `padding-left: 50%`, which effectively ignores `max-w-[80px]` and breaks text truncation, potentially causing long destination strings to overflow or clip awkwardly against the pseudo-element label on mobile devices.

The mobile CSS unconditionally forces `text-align: right !important` and `justify-content: flex-end` on all `td` elements. In Reports.tsx, fields like 'FORMULA', 'CROP/USE', 'UNIT', etc., were previously left-aligned. This unconditional override degrades the UX by right-aligning data that is semantically categorical rather than numeric.

The mobile-card CSS relies on `content: attr(data-label)` on `<td>` elements to render labels. The FSA table in Reports.tsx has 4 rows (lines 653-656 in context) used as a nested header (e.g., 'Fall Harvest Crops'). Because CSS forces all tr/td to display:block on mobile, these header rows lose their th context. More importantly, other tables in this file likely render `<td>` elements without data-label attributes via the generic ReportTable component, causing empty labels on mobile.

The CSS selector `.mobile-cards tr` uses a descendant combinator that matches ALL <tr> elements within the container, including nested table headers. Worse, because ReportTable is a generic component, any row rendered WITHOUT a data-label attribute will be visually broken on mobile (empty ::before pseudo-element). This causes silent data corruption for any existing table consumers that were not updated to include data-label attributes.

row.production.toLocaleString() is locale-dependent. While not a crash, if the user's locale uses non-Latin digits (e.g., Arabic-Indic numerals ١٢٣) or different group separators, it can produce unexpected strings in a compliance report context. Consider explicit locale enforcement.

The mobile-cards CSS converts <td> cells into display:flex blocks, and the table rows (tr) become cards. In Reports.tsx the action/hover rows and any cell-level interaction now render as flex rows with min-height:2.5rem (40px). Per AGENTS.md mobile-first rule, touch targets should be >= 44px. With 0.5rem card padding plus cell spacing the actual hit height per row may fall below 44px on small screens.

row.production.toLocaleString() is called unconditionally. If row.production is null/undefined/NaN (adversarial import or partial record), toLocaleString yields 'NaN'/'null' rather than the '—' placeholder used everywhere else in the row. A null production value will render the literal string 'null' instead of an em-dash, inconsistent with the null-handling contract of the surrounding cells.

`row.production.toLocaleString()` will throw `TypeError: Cannot read properties of null/undefined (reading 'toLocaleString')` when `row.production` is null or undefined. No null guard like the other cells (`row.moisturePercent != null ? ... : '—'`). A row with missing production data crashes the entire Reports page render. Fix: `{row.production != null ? row.production.toLocaleString() : '—'}`.

The mobile card layout relies on the `:has(td[colspan])` selector to de-style rows like 'No records' empty-state rows, but `:has()` is unsupported in older browsers. On Firefox <121, Safari <15.4, and all legacy Chromium those full-width rows render as bordered cards with the empty-state label visually broken.

`white-space: nowrap` on `td::before` in the mobile card layout means long data-labels (e.g., 'DEST/STORAGE', 'EVIDENCE #', 'TYPE/VARIETY') will overflow and be clipped because the label column is constrained to 45% width with no overflow handling.

Uses `:has(td[colspan])` selector to target empty-state/spinner rows. `:has()` is not supported in older Safari/Chrome versions, causing these rows to render as full cards with broken labels on mobile in those browsers.

The 'Total Operation' summary block is injected at the top of the fragment with 'mt-4', but it relies on 'filteredRowCrops.length > 0' below it. If `cropTotals.length > 0` but `filteredRowCrops.length === 0` (all crops filtered out), the summary block renders orphaned at the top of the page, discarding the sticky bottom behavior the original code intended to fix.
`tr:has(td[colspan])` relies on the `:has()` selector, which is unsupported in older Safari/legacy mobile browsers, leading to a complete layout breakage for 'No records found' rows.

The crop toggle button height was reduced from `h-10` (40px) to `h-9` (36px). The project's accessibility rules require touch targets to be at least 44px high (`h-11`), making this a regression on mobile usability.
